Gym winners, welcome back. So you should have just gone through the diagnosing, selling script, feel pretty good about it. This is a bolt-on that I wanted to add in. I contemplated, because I don't like making any additional trainings if I don't have to. But the results that came from one of our gyms was so outstanding and so compelling, I felt obligated to make this. So this comes from a gym that's in Las Vegas, Normal Town, and they were getting 80% of their clients to prepay. So pay in full upfront at an average upfront payment of $2,800. And these were normal people coming in for 42-day detoxes. So nothing crazy. And the way that they were able to do this was through a series of questions that they took from the bottom of the clothes and just a smooth transition to how they explain the value and then also how they close the credit card. I'm going to walk you through it. So at the end of the diagnostic shift, you say, hey, do you have any questions? Where would you like to go from here? And then they're going to say, oh, well, how does it work? What's the price? And you're going to say, awesome. So given your goal, Susan, of losing 30 pounds, it's going to take you time. It's going to take you, so if you want to lose 30 pounds, it's going to take you 15 weeks to lose it, but then probably another maybe 12-ish weeks on the back end. So let's just call it 24 to hit your goal. And I can guarantee that we're going to get you there. So the next question is, how quickly you want to achieve it? And so the easiest way that we like to explain this is just like when you're picking your vacation destination. So your vacation destination is we're losing 30 pounds. You, Susan, are going to go from 160 to 130. So it's what type of flight do you want to take on your island to 130? So you can take a private jet to the island, which is 100% customized to you, one-on-one personal training. Here at the gym, you have one person with you, one person holding you count from nutrition, one person for accountability. It's all individualized to you. And that's $750 a week. The next level is $99 a week, which is just flying first class. You're still going to have all the individual attention. It's just when you work out here, you're going to be with some other people. The next level beyond that is 77 a week for economy. And below that, you have 49 a week for the party bus. The party bus, a lot of people have fun, but it's going to take you a lot longer to hit your goal. So given what you told me, I think that 99 a week for the first class, which is going to be group plus hybrid, is going to be your best bet. So if you're saying group plus height, which is a group plus, one of our nutrition accountability, is going to be your best bet. So which one do you think makes the most sense for you? And then they're going to probably pick this one, which is the majority of the time of what they're going to select. And then you're going to say, awesome. So given what you just told me, it's going to take us 24 weeks to get to the goal. So because you're taking first class, we're going to get your goal faster, but we're going to spend a longer period of time rebuilding and toning so that once you get your goal weight, you look even better. And you can actually eat more by the end of this program that you are right now at a lower weight feeling more tone. Sound great? Fantastic. All right. So what most people do, so this is when you go to the close, you're like, great. Sound great? Awesome. So what most people do is they prepay and save 20%, right? Fair enough? They're going to say awesome. Or they're not. So what I want to highlight here is where it says fair enough. You always want to say fair enough, sound fair. And the reason for that is because when you say, sound so, I actually made one mistake. I said, sound great. You don't want to say that. You want to say sounds fair. Is you want to make sure that you're trying to get them to say yes to the lowest bar, right? Does this sound like the best thing you've ever done in your life? They might be like, no, it doesn't, right? But does it sound fair? Sounds fair. Because as soon as they say yes, that's the question that you, if they say yes, you've closed them. You just say awesome. What card do you want to use? Let's do it, right? Now, if for some reason you say, what most people do is they prepay to save 20%, right? Fair enough? They're going to say, OK. Or like, sound fair? They'll say yes, whatever. Now, if for some reason they're heeing and hawing, and you can read their body language, and it doesn't look like they're digging it, they're freaked out, you're like, no worries. What we do here is we have two different types of payment plans. You can use your hands. So either we can choose how much you pay and when you pay it, or you can choose how much you pay and when you pay it every month. What works best for you? They're going to say, right? I want to pick. And you're going to say, great choice. Most people just put it on a credit card so they can grab the 20% savings and then pay on their own budget and schedule. Fair enough? And they're going to say, OK. As soon as they do that, you'll take their credit card and you'll still do a pay in full. So it's like, you want to pay in full? I'm not sure. Great. Most people just use a credit card and pay in full. But you just have to explain to them that when they use a card, they can pay on their own budget and schedule. Those are the two variables. Budget, how much they pay, schedule when they pay. And so after they do that, again, you're going to say, sound fair? Fair enough? And they're going to say, OK. Now if at that point they're like, I'm not sure. I don't have that much room on my card. And you're like, awesome. So how much room do you have in your card? And be like, I only have 1,000. Awesome. So what I can actually do since you've been close to half down, I can give you the 10% prepay benefit. So you can still snag that 10% off just for prepaying half. And on top of that, you get some savings on all the payments you're going to have from here on out. Sound fair? Sound fair? They're like, yeah, it sounds fair. Also notice the tonality. Sound fair? Not like, sound fair? Like, sound fair? Like, you have to raise your voice at the end so that you get a response. And so they stay engaged in the conversation. Now from there, if you can't close half down, then you're just going to move down. So normally you don't want to change the service level that you've just diagnosed them and that they agreed that they wanted. You just want to change what the payment that they're coming down to is.
